subject: Chemistry
title: The Deepwater Horizon Spill
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Deepwater Horizon Spill.

hook.text: In 2010, the Deepwater Horizon oil rig exploded, killing 11 workers and releasing 780 million liters of oil into the Gulf.

buildup.text: A blowout preventer failed, and high-pressure oil and gas surged up the drill pipe from 1,500 meters below the seafloor.

discovery.text: BP used 7 million liters of chemical dispersant to break up the oil. The dispersant itself proved toxic to marine life.

twist.text: The well leaked for 87 days before being capped. It was the largest accidental marine oil spill in history.

climax.text: BP paid $65 billion in cleanup costs and fines. The spill devastated Gulf fishing and tourism for years.

punchline.text: A failed valve, 87 days of gushing oil. The cure was almost as toxic as the spill.

quiz.question: What problem arose from using chemical dispersants on the Deepwater Horizon spill?
options: The dispersant itself proved toxic to marine life
The dispersant caused the oil to solidify and sink
Dispersants attracted more oil from neighboring wells
